description:
## Outcome
A product's workstreams exist with goal.md, backlog, and CLAUDE.md wiring per workstream. The bootstrap step that enables all downstream work.

## JTBD
Bootstrap (enables all JTBDs)

## Eval Targets
guard-001 (no eval duplication in goal.md/CLAUDE.md)

## FR-Level Evals
- FR-001a: Given a product with strategy.md and evals.json, when the onboarding skill runs, then 2-3 fracture plane options are proposed with rationale
- FR-001b: Given the user selects a fracture plane, when confirmed, then goal.md + ideas-backlog.md + .claude/CLAUDE.md are created per workstream
- FR-001c: Given workstreams are created, when Claude Code enters a workstream directory, then CLAUDE.md correctly points to goal.md, evals, and backlog

## Adaptation Boundaries
CAN: Split/merge tasks, add evals, reorder tasks
CANNOT: Delete FR-evals, skip quality gate, change JTBD scope
ESCALATE: If >8 tasks needed, or architecture conflict
title: [Epic] Workstream Onboarding

description:
## Outcome
Scheduled agent runs experiments autonomously at 5am DK. Picks from backlog, implements in worktree, runs evals, ships or rejects. Results written to Supabase.

## JTBD
Execute experiments on a heartbeat

## Eval Targets
li-001 >= 0.60 (ship rate: 3+ experiments/week)
li-003 >= 0.80 (autonomy ratio: 80% of runs without human intervention)

## FR-Level Evals
- FR-003a: Given a scheduled trigger at 5am DK, when agent starts, then it reads goal.md and picks top hypothesis from ideas-backlog.md
- FR-003b: Given a hypothesis is selected, then agent creates worktree, implements, runs tests, runs evals in Ralph Wiggum loop (max 3 retries per error)
- FR-003c: Given an experiment completes, then results are written to Supabase with status, eval deltas, branch, timestamp, qualitative description
- FR-003d: Given an experiment fails evals or hits circuit breaker, then change is rejected and learnings logged

## Negative Cases
- Agent hallucinated the fix: experiment ships but builder marks "wouldn't care if reverted" (score: 0.1)

## Circuit Breakers
- 3 same-error failures -> STOP
- >5 files touched -> STOP
- Longer than estimated -> STOP and escalate

## Adaptation Boundaries
CAN: Split/merge tasks, add evals, reorder tasks
CANNOT: Delete FR-evals, skip quality gate, weaken eval targets
ESCALATE: If /schedule doesn't work, fall back to Vercel Cron
title: [Epic] Execution Engine

description:
## What
Build the autonomous experiment loop: worktree → implement → test → eval → fix/retry → ship or reject.

## Why
Moves FR-003b: Agent creates worktree, implements, runs tests, runs evals in loop with circuit breakers.
This is the core of the execution engine — the thing that makes products improve while you sleep.

## Context
- Files: experiment runner script/skill
- Prior tasks: Scheduled agent trigger
- Shared resources: product codebase (via worktree), evals

## Done When
- [ ] Creates git worktree for isolated experiment
- [ ] Implements the hypothesis change
- [ ] Runs test suite
- [ ] Runs eval suite, measures before/after deltas
- [ ] On test failure: fix and retry (max 3 attempts per error)
- [ ] On eval improvement: merge worktree, mark as shipped
- [ ] On eval regression or no improvement: reject, log learnings
- [ ] Circuit breaker: 3 same-error failures → STOP
- [ ] Circuit breaker: >5 files touched → STOP
- [ ] Quality gate: typecheck && lint && test all pass

## Eval Spec
Given: A hypothesis "simplify onboarding to 3 steps" is selected
When: The experiment runner executes
Then: It creates a worktree, implements the change, runs evals, and ships or rejects based on eval delta

## Circuit Breakers
- 3 same-error test failures → STOP, create STUCK bead
- >5 files touched → STOP, create SPLIT bead
- Longer than estimated → STOP and escalate
title: Ralph Wiggum experiment runner

description:
Run the complete eval suite. Verify the full loop: capture → enrich → execute → review.

## Evals to run
- eval-001: Capture frequency (target: 0.85)
- eval-002: Thread depth (target: 0.70)
- eval-003: Inspiration loop (target: 0.60)
- eval-004: Active review engagement (target: 0.70)
- li-001: Ship rate (target: 0.60)
- li-002: Backlog velocity (target: 0.80)
- li-003: Autonomy ratio (target: 0.80)
- guard-001: No eval duplication
- guard-002: One-click revert works

## Replan Assessment
Full assessment. If any eval below 50% of target, escalate.
title: [Checkpoint] Full eval suite — end-to-end

description:
## What
Configure Claude Code Channels to listen on workstream-specific Discord channels. Verify messages are received. Test on mobile.

## Why
Moves FR-002a: Message received via Channels within 60s.
This is the highest-risk task — if Channels doesn't support async listening, fall back to Chat SDK bot.

## Context
- Files: Claude Code Channels config, Discord server setup
- Prior tasks: CLAUDE.md wiring template (Epic 1 complete)
- Shared resources: Discord server (shared with all workstreams)

## Done When
- [ ] Discord server created with one channel per workstream
- [ ] Claude Code Channels configured to listen on each channel
- [ ] Test message sent from mobile → Claude Code receives it within 60s
- [ ] Quality gate: typecheck && lint && test all pass

## Eval Spec
Given: A Discord channel #improve-onboarding exists
When: User sends "I think the welcome screen is too long"
Then: Claude Code receives the message via Channels within 60 seconds

## Guardrails
- If Channels doesn't support persistent async listening: ESCALATE. Create fallback task for Chat SDK bot.

## Circuit Breakers
- 3 test failures on same error → STOP
title: Discord Channels setup

description:
## What
Build the graduation step: user signals completion, system synthesizes thread into structured hypothesis, appends to ideas-backlog.md.

## Why
Moves FR-002c+d: Graduate to structured hypothesis with all required fields.

## Context
- Files: graduation logic, ideas-backlog.md
- Prior tasks: Sparring conversation flow
- Shared resources: ideas-backlog.md (written to)

## Done When
- [ ] User can signal graduation ("port it", "graduate", or similar)
- [ ] System synthesizes thread into structured hypothesis
- [ ] Hypothesis includes: problem statement, proposed change, target eval(s), expected delta, risks, experiment design
- [ ] Hypothesis appended to ideas-backlog.md
- [ ] Quality gate: typecheck && lint && test all pass

## Eval Spec
Given: A sparring thread with 5+ exchanges about "simplify onboarding"
When: User says "graduate this"
Then: ideas-backlog.md has a new entry with all required fields

## Circuit Breakers
- 3 test failures on same error → STOP
title: Hypothesis graduation to backlog

description:
## Outcome
Persistent Next.js dashboard on Vercel showing experiment results, eval deltas, qualitative narratives, try-it-now links, Sean Ellis feedback, one-click revert, and eval trend lines.

## JTBD
Review results with confidence

## Eval Targets
eval-004 >= 0.70 (active review: click-through + feedback on 70% of results)
guard-002 (one-click revert from dashboard)

## FR-Level Evals
- FR-004a: Given experiments have run, when dashboard loads, then results show with qualitative narrative, eval deltas, workstream filter
- FR-004b: Given a result is shown, then it includes a direct link to try the changed product
- FR-004c: Given a result is shown, then Sean Ellis feedback form is available inline
- FR-004d: Given user clicks "revert", then Claude Code is triggered to revert the specific experiment
- FR-004e: Given experiment touched multiple changes, then granular per-change revert buttons are available

## Adaptation Boundaries
CAN: Split/merge tasks, add evals, reorder tasks
CANNOT: Delete FR-evals, skip quality gate, remove revert functionality
ESCALATE: If revert triggers Claude Code unreliably
title: [Epic] Review Dashboard

description:
## What
Set up Claude Code /schedule to trigger at 5am DK time. Agent enters workstream directory, reads goal.md, picks top hypothesis from backlog.

## Why
Moves FR-003a: Scheduled trigger reads goal.md and picks top hypothesis.

## Context
- Files: /schedule configuration, workstream directories
- Prior tasks: Supabase schema
- Shared resources: ideas-backlog.md (read), goal.md (read)

## Done When
- [ ] /schedule configured for 5am DK (3am UTC in summer, 4am UTC in winter)
- [ ] Agent enters correct workstream directory
- [ ] Agent reads goal.md for context
- [ ] Agent picks top hypothesis from ideas-backlog.md by priority
- [ ] Test trigger works manually
- [ ] Quality gate: typecheck && lint && test all pass

## Eval Spec
Given: ideas-backlog.md has 3 queued hypotheses with different priorities
When: The scheduled trigger fires
Then: Agent picks the highest-priority hypothesis and logs selection

## Guardrails
- If /schedule doesn't work: ESCALATE. Fall back to Vercel Cron.

## Circuit Breakers
- 3 test failures on same error → STOP
title: Scheduled agent trigger at 5am DK

description:
Run li-001 and li-003. Verify experiments ship and autonomy ratio holds.

## Evals to run
- li-001: Ship rate ≥0.60 (3+ experiments/week)
- li-003: Autonomy ratio ≥0.80 (80% runs without human intervention)
- eval-001, eval-002: Verify no regression from prior checkpoint

## Replan Assessment
After running evals, answer:
1. Did any eval score diverge >20% from the plan's estimate?
2. Did implementation reveal capabilities the plan missed?
3. Did implementation reveal the plan assumed something wrong?

If YES to any: Create REPLAN bead, flag for human review.
If NO to all: Continue with Epic 4.
title: [Checkpoint] Eval suite after Execution Engine

close_reason: Fracture plane analysis module complete. 4 files: package.json, tsconfig.json, src/fracture-planes.ts, src/fracture-planes.test.ts. Reads strategy.md + evals.json, proposes 3 planes (by-jtbd, by-journey-phase, by-technical-surface) with rationale and trade-offs. Fuzzy JTBD matching handles frontmatter/eval title mismatches. 19/19 tests pass, typecheck clean.
description:
## What
Build the logic that reads strategy JTBDs, evals, and codebase to propose 2-3 genuinely different ways to slice a product into workstreams.

## Why
Moves FR-001a: Given strategy + evals, propose 2-3 fracture planes with trade-offs.
Without this, workstreams must be manually defined — defeating the Autonomy principle.

## Context
- Files: strategy.md, evals.json, product codebase
- APIs: none
- Prior tasks: none (first task)
- Shared resources: strategy.md (read-only)

## Done When
- [ ] Reads strategy.md and evals.json
- [ ] Proposes 2-3 fracture planes (e.g., by JTBD, by journey phase, by technical surface)
- [ ] Each option includes rationale and trade-offs
- [ ] Quality gate: typecheck && lint && test all pass

## Eval Spec
Given: A product with strategy.md containing 3+ JTBDs and evals.json
When: The onboarding skill runs
Then: 2-3 fracture plane options are presented with rationale

## Circuit Breakers
- 3 test failures on same error → STOP
- >5 files → STOP, task too broad
title: Fracture plane analysis skill

description:
## What
Build the enrichment logic: 2-3 clarifying questions, eval mapping, expected delta estimation. Reads goal.md for strategic context.

## Why
Moves FR-002b: Ask clarifying questions, map to evals, estimate delta.
This is the core of "Seamless Entry, Enriched Output" — the system asks good enough questions that the builder voluntarily keeps going.

## Context
- Files: sparring skill source, goal.md (read), evals.md (read by reference)
- Prior tasks: Idea capture skill
- Shared resources: goal.md (read-only), evals.md (read-only, never copied)

## Done When
- [ ] System asks 2-3 clarifying questions in the thread
- [ ] Questions reference goal.md direction for this workstream
- [ ] System maps idea to relevant evals (by reference)
- [ ] System estimates expected eval delta
- [ ] guard-001: no eval content copied into the thread or backlog
- [ ] Quality gate: typecheck && lint && test all pass

## Eval Spec
Given: User captured an idea about "simplify onboarding"
When: Claude Code responds in the thread
Then: It asks clarifying questions that reference the onboarding workstream's goal.md direction

## Circuit Breakers
- 3 test failures on same error → STOP
- >5 files → STOP

description:
Run eval-001 and eval-002 with test data. Verify capture and enrichment work end-to-end.

## Evals to run
- eval-001: Builder captures ≥2 ideas/day (target: 0.85)
- eval-002: Thread depth ≥5 exchanges (target: 0.70)

## Replan Assessment
After running evals, answer:
1. Did any eval score diverge >20% from the plan's estimate?
2. Did implementation reveal capabilities the plan missed?
3. Did implementation reveal the plan assumed something wrong?

If YES to any: Create bd create --title="REPLAN: [what changed]" --type=task --priority=0 -l replan
If NO to all: Continue with Epic 3.

## Regression check
- guard-001 (no eval duplication) must still pass
title: [Checkpoint] Eval suite after Capture & Enrich

description:
## What
Revert button per experiment. Granular per-change revert when experiment touched multiple things. Triggers Claude Code to execute the actual revert.

## Why
Moves guard-002: One-click revert from dashboard.
This is the safety net that enables bold experimentation — ship fast, revert cheaply.

## Context
- Files: dashboard components, revert API route
- Prior tasks: Experiment results view
- Shared resources: Product codebase (via Claude Code revert)

## Done When
- [ ] Revert button renders per shipped experiment
- [ ] Clicking revert triggers Claude Code to revert the specific commit/branch
- [ ] Granular per-change revert buttons available when experiment touched multiple files
- [ ] Reverted experiment status updates in Supabase
- [ ] No terminal access required
- [ ] Quality gate: typecheck && lint && test all pass

## Eval Spec
Given: A shipped experiment with 3 file changes
When: User clicks "Revert change 2"
Then: Claude Code reverts only that change, Supabase status updates

## Circuit Breakers
- 3 test failures on same error → STOP
title: One-click revert from dashboard

description:
## Outcome
Builder captures ideas via Discord and the system enriches them through conversation into structured hypotheses in the backlog.

## JTBD
Capture ideas instantly + Enrich ideas through conversation

## Eval Targets
eval-001 >= 0.85 (capture frequency: 2+ ideas/day, 6 of 7 days)
eval-002 >= 0.70 (thread depth: 70% of threads reach 5+ exchanges)

## FR-Level Evals
- FR-002a: Given a Discord channel per workstream, when user sends a message, then Claude Code picks it up via Channels within 60s
- FR-002b: Given an idea is captured, when Claude Code responds, then it opens a thread and asks a clarifying question within 60s
- FR-002c: Given a sparring conversation reaches conclusion, when user signals graduation, then structured hypothesis is appended to ideas-backlog.md
- FR-002d: Given a hypothesis is graduated, then it includes: problem statement, proposed change, target eval(s), expected delta, risks, experiment design

## Negative Cases
- Ideas into the void: captured but <50% receive enrichment thread within 1h (score: 0.2)
- Scattered, no story: <30% of hypotheses reference goal.md direction (score: 0.2)

## Adaptation Boundaries
CAN: Split/merge tasks, add evals, reorder tasks
CANNOT: Delete FR-evals, skip quality gate, change JTBD scope
ESCALATE: If Channels doesn't support async Discord listening — fall back to Chat SDK bot
title: [Epic] Capture & Enrich Pipeline

close_reason: CLAUDE.md template enhanced with experiment workflow, adaptation boundaries, circuit breakers. guard-001 verified: no eval content in CLAUDE.md or goal.md. 5 new tests, 32/32 total pass.
description:

Write the CLAUDE.md template that correctly wires Claude Code to goal.md, evals (by reference), backlog, and adaptation boundaries.

## Why

Also enforces guard-001: evals referenced, never copied.

## Context

- Prior tasks: Directory scaffolding
- Shared resources: evals.md (referenced, never copied)

## Done When
- [ ] CLAUDE.md points to goal.md for direction
- [ ] CLAUDE.md references evals.md path (never copies content)
- [ ] CLAUDE.md points to ideas-backlog.md for hypothesis queue
- [ ] Claude Code entering the directory picks up correct context
- [ ] guard-001: grep confirms no eval content in CLAUDE.md
- [ ] Quality gate: typecheck && lint && test all pass

## Eval Spec
Given: Claude Code enters workstreams/onboarding/
When: It reads .claude/CLAUDE.md
Then: It knows the workstream direction, where evals live, and where the backlog is

## Circuit Breakers
- 3 test failures on same error → STOP
title: CLAUDE.md wiring template
